title: Microsoft announces layoff of 3,200 Xbox employees and divestment of several studios
summary: The most significant restructuring in Xbox history includes the closure of four studios and the departure of others, in an attempt to refocus the business.

title: Debate over the end of physical format intensifies with criticism from Kojima and politicians, while Xbox defends discs
summary: Sony reduces disc production, but Xbox confirms Halo in physical form and figures like Kojima warn about the loss of ownership in video games.

title: Nintendo announces discontinuation of original Switch in Europe and prepares Switch 2 revisions with replaceable battery
summary: Production of the original console will end in February 2027, while the new Switch 2 models will feature replaceable batteries.

title: Xbox CEO sets an unrealistic goal: entertaining a billion people a day
summary: After massive layoffs, Asha Sharma acknowledges the company overreached, but sets a difficult growth target.

title: Xbox layoffs hit Obsidian, Bethesda, Id Software, and ZeniMax Online hard
summary: Key studios lose veterans and key personnel as the company restructures its focus and prioritizes major franchises.

title: Xbox Game Pass fails to meet expectations: subscriptions drop and CEO admits strategy hasn't worked
summary: Subscriber numbers drop to 30 million, well below the targets announced by Microsoft.

title: Experts and developers criticize catastrophic layoffs at Xbox, calling them a stressful annual routine
summary: The industry reacts with anger and concern over massive cuts, pointing to mismanagement and growing job insecurity.

title: ZeniMax refocuses strategy to center on Fallout, The Elder Scrolls, and other star franchises
summary: Xbox restructuring forces ZeniMax to concentrate its resources on its most important franchises, without ruling out Doom and Quake.

title: Double Fine, Compulsion, and other studios break away from Xbox and regain independence
summary: As part of the restructuring, several first-party Xbox studios are sold or regain their autonomy, while others seek a buyer.

title: Marvel's Blade and Arkane's future up in the air: founder jokes about buying back the studio after layoffs
summary: The French studio is temporarily saved from cuts by French law, but its continuity and the Blade game are in doubt.

title: New viral outbreak in World of Warcraft revives legendary 'Corrupted Blood Incident'
summary: A bug in a WoW dungeon causes a plague that spreads among players, recalling one of the most famous events in the MMO.

title: Summer Games Done Quick 2026 kicks off with records and debut of Kirby Air Riders
summary: The annual speedrunning marathon raises funds for cancer research with charity runs and new title reveals.

title: Katsuhiro Harada defends Miyazaki and Sakurai: Don't judge games by their sales
summary: The veteran Tekken producer criticizes evaluating video games based on sales figures and praises the careers of Japanese developers.

title: Cyberpunk: Edgerunners 2 debuts to positive reviews and CD Projekt Red thanks fans for their discretion
summary: The second season of the animated series receives excellent reviews, and the studio notes that early viewers did not spoil it.

title: Steam game racks up 55,000 refunds for its short length, but maintains very positive reviews
summary: The developer of 'Paddle Paddle Paddle' explains that most refunds are because the game lasts less than two hours.

title: Persona 4: The Animation released for free on YouTube to celebrate the franchise's revival
summary: Fans can enjoy the acclaimed anime adaptation for free and officially on the company's YouTube channel.

title: The dreaded red line error on Steam Machines has a fix and isn't as serious as thought
summary: Several users report that the graphical error that scared Steam Machine owners can be easily fixed.

title: Marvel Tokon: Fighting Souls receives high praise but suffers from serious regional lock issues on PSN
summary: The new Marvel fighting game is critically acclaimed, although over 130 countries cannot access it due to Sony restrictions.

title: Nintendo named best developer of the year by Famitsu and Square Enix plans to bring Final Fantasy to Switch 2
summary: The Japanese company receives a major accolade, while Square Enix considers launching one of its most divisive Final Fantasy titles on the new console.

title: Xbox confirms Halo: Campaign Evolved will be released in physical format amid debate over discs
summary: The company takes the side of players who prefer physical format, confirming that the Halo remake will have a disc version.
